interpretation_text: PVP (preço de venda ao público) in Infomed is the Portuguese public retail price. It maps to comparison_category=public_retail_price.
adjudication_notes: Infomed terms state that use is free and registration-free, while downstream reproduction/distribution of the database requires authorization. This project treats the captured extract as internal research evidence and does not republish the raw Infomed database.
includes_margin: public retail price including VAT and applicable public retail components
caveats: Infomed is a JSF search application rather than a simple bulk file; captures should preserve query/export evidence.
This lane should only be compared with other public_retail_price lanes unless an explicit derivation rule bridges margins/VAT.

notes: Infomed is a JSF search application rather than a simple bulk file; captures should preserve query/export evidence.
This lane should only be compared with other public_retail_price lanes unless an explicit derivation rule bridges margins/VAT.

source_references: Infarmed — Margens de Comercialização e Fatores de Conversão
interpretation_text: Derived PVA/ex-factory price from Portuguese Infomed PVP using Infarmed commercial margin tiers, fixed fees, commercialisation tax treatment and 6% VAT conversion formulas. It maps to comparison_category=manufacturer_price and is generated as a virtual price lane with a DerivationRule, not by mutating the observed public retail record.
adjudication_notes: The tier is selected by the derived PVA interval. Confidential discounts/rebates and non-standard arrangements are not observable.
includes_margin: no standard wholesale/pharmacy margins after derivation
caveats: Derived lane; always inspect the linked DerivationRule before headline use.
The current PT raw snapshot is a targeted research extract, not a full national bulk capture.

notes: Derived lane; always inspect the linked DerivationRule before headline use.

legal_basis: Infarmed Margens de Comercialização e Fatores de Conversão
